reviews.review_text: The cabin we were given on arrival was simply unpresentable. There were crumbs on the table, shopping tags on the couch, dirt on the sink and no spare toilet paper  rolls. When the girl came to give the cabin another clean, she dropped cleaning fluid on the floor, which wasn't wiped up properly. On cleaning the rest myself, I noticed the floor was very dirty and hadn't been moped since god knows when. I also noticed hair on the floor.||After complaining again we were given a cabin upgrade, much to their credit. However, while the next cabin was better, it was still of a poor standard. We swept up quite a bit of dirt on the floor and noticed quite a few cobwebs scattered around.||Would not recommend.

On our arrival at Waterloo Bay Tourist Park in Elliston SA, there was a sign on the door saying "Open". However, the door was locked and a note to ring. After ringing the advertised number 4 times with no answer, decided to choose our own site. NO WELCOME, FILTHY AMENITIES, FILTHY CAMP KITCHEN (nothing cleaned for the 2 days/ nights we were there) & NO DUMP POINT. Positive notes - level sites, close to everything & great water pressure.
Wat een verschil met port Augusta. Aan de balie ongeïnteresseerd. Het sanitair is niet echt schoon. Het stinkt naar urine. De campkichen is een aanfluiting. En dat voor 40 dollar.
Fantastic location, cabin we stayed in need a good deep clean, very very small.
Amenities were pretty grubby and not well maintained at all including the camp kitchen and its seating. No lighting and only one washing machine and dryer working out of the 4 available.
Nice little park close to the beach we could walk to all the services

What a absolute dump
Day two here and amenities haven't been cleaned
Men's toilet and shower area stink of urine
Location is fabulous. Close to beach ⛱️

We had 3 nights recently in this caravan park and have been coming here with friends from Port Lincoln for many years.
We were disappointed with the general cleanliness of amenities and surrounds and feel the park needs a refresh.

The whole cabin smelled like strong of males urine on every surface and no soap provided, found one cake of soap under the bed and some other guest had been "eating mayo sandwiches" on the couch 🤮 with evidence left crusty and it was all over the mirror too.
The only good thing is the free go carts for use around the park. Rude staff. Great location.
Snugly sites if your a big rig but otherwise good value considering the price u can pay elsewhere.  Squid n fish to be caught at jetty but put ya walking feet on its about 2km away. Nice beach walk
nice small park near swimming beach. good pub, bakery and shop in town . I would recommend staying here

This has been our favourite park in one of our favourite locations for the past 18+ years. The location is perfect tucked in behind the dunes of the most beautiful beach and an easy walk to the shops/pub.
Unfortunately, the cleaning and maintenance of the facilities at our recent stay left a lot to be desired for the first time ever. No soap in the ladies toilets for 4 out of 8 days, the camp kitchen wasn’t cleaned the entire time we were there and only one of the 3 bbqs worked at all. Hoping this improves so we can come back again with our extended families in the future.
Had a caravan site,camp kitchen was great , showers were disappointing cold water when even you put the hot water on full

Peter is very helpful.
Had a night at budget cabin. Was clean, small but given the price we are happy with it.
Recommend for one night.
The only annoying part was there were loud people partying on at 11pm with loud music and no one seemed to stop them. Should not be happening in a caravan park like that.
A lovely old-fashioned caravan park, in town, beside the beach. Friendly, helpful owners and good facilities. A great place to spend Christmas with our family.

Well, do I start. We booked and paid for a cabin on the 4th December for the 31/12/25 & 1/1/26. We received a phone call at 8:30pm on the 24/12/25 from them to tell us they have cancelled our booking as they cannot accommodate us & proceeded to blame Expedia, the platform we booked through by saying they don’t what’s going on with them!

Firstly, if it was a booking issue from the start, they should have been in touch the day or two after we made our booking. Not 3 weeks later & at 8:30pm on Christmas Eve when there is no chance of booking elsewhere. We had set plans for our time in Elliston which is why we booked in the first place. Is no good to suggest to us we book for another time as they did. If we wanted another time we would have booked for that time.

All good we are getting a refund, on Christmas Eve with a public holiday the next day and the weekend following which means the refund won’t come in until sometime next week. So not only is there no other accommodation available, we don’t have the funds to pay for it!

So so very unprofessional, disgraceful and disrespectful. They would have known the day or two after our booking if they couldn’t accommodate us. So what happened? The people before us wanted to extend and rather than so no, we are booked, they cancel us and completely ruin our holiday?

We didn’t receive a call or email until tonight, even though they said they tried to contact us. That is complete BS. Very bad poor form and extremely unprofessional. I would never treat a customer this way, especially one that had already paid.

I would give zero stars if it was an option. One star is being super generous. And just to think, we would’ve been yearly repeat customers. To say we are extremely upset is an understatement.
Very good service

We felt this was a very average caravan park as it was old and really in need of a renovation. The site we were allocated was very small and there was no sullage point. A lot of the area was worn out and covered with stones. The area however was very nice as was the local hotel where you get great meals.

Booked an ensuite site for our caravan stay and now after reading many previous reviews I wish we hadn’t. On arrival at 2pm the lady was not at the office which was shut with an old sign which says “back in 15 minutes” stuck on the door. I finally found an intercom so I pushed the button, no answer from the speaker and after 10 minutes a lady casually wandered over, opened the door, sat at the desk, looked at me and said nothing. I told her my name and I was checking in. No response until she handed me a sheet of paper and a jey and said “number 2 just there” and got up to leave the office. We have a big van with slid-out and the site was so small we couldn’t put the slide out fully out.  She knew we had this setup as we booked online and it asks. Then we saw the ensuite, it was filthy, soap skum and toothpaste all over the walls and vanity, floor filthy and rubbish bins with old rubbish in them. There was also a major earwig and ant problem at the doorway where hundreds of them were running around in the ensuite. I got spray and ant powder from my own supplies and treated them.went back to “reception” no one there so I pushed the intercom as before. Waited 10 minutes nobody came so I called the emergency number listed on the door. Peter answered and I told him our issue and he said he will said someone over. 15 minutes later the office lady arrived and said “what’s the problem” I showed her the filth and the many earwigs and ants running everywhere and showed her how I had treated them. She said under her breath “this hasn’t been cleaned, sorry about that” and walked off. We had to use the main ammenities as she took forever and after she left the soap skum was still on the shower walls. Nothing done about the ant problem as they were still coming in. Avoid this park at all costs and keep driving. Poorly maintained grounds as well. We have not seen a worse park and rude hosts in our 6 months of travelling. How it can be listed on the G’Day site surprises me.

We decided to stay at this G’day park and it was a bit of a disappointment.
Booked and paid for 2 nights but only stayed the one.
Just had a weird vibe.
The manager was not very interested in giving any information about the park or town.
It all had a very unloved look.
Bathrooms ok but no evidence they were cleaned daily.
The path to the foreshore was overgrown and the bbq area horrible at best.
Such a shame but if going to Elliston the hotel is the best feature and perhaps stay at the other park.

Nice caravan park.
Good sized sites and good facilities.
Right opposite Oyster HQ
Very nice and quiet caravan park. Clean amenities. 👍
Unfriendly staff amenities need cleaning

We booked a two-day stay at this caravan park our experience has been disappointing. Upon arrival, I received minimal greeting, and we were not provided with directions to our site or information about the amenities, laundry, or camp kitchen or information about the town. We were simply given a piece of paper highlighting our site number from reception no direction no enjoy your stay nothing!
We were also disappointed to find that there is no dump point, and the sullage waste point (a small drain) which is located behind the toilet block, near the dishwashing bench, which is not hygienic! there is also a sign above this drain that says to check at reception before pouring anything down it.
Don't even worry about using the camp kitchen its extremely unclean! cob webs everywhere including dirty dusty tables and chairs that look like they haven't been used for months even years, the amenities were not overly clean as well this to me highlights lack of maintenance. Additionally, the campground sites are not well-lit, very dark at night making it difficult to navigate to the amenities in the dark. I do not recommend this park!
We cut our loses and left this grotty place a day early, I did email my concerns and lack of cleanliness requesting a refund however I did not get a response back no surprises there.
As a G'day park this lady managing/owns the show really needs to do better or get out of the industry if you hate your job that much! I really believe this woman  doesn't even care about her customers she really needs to open her eyes and not forget without us she won't have a business.
Steer clear people

It's not up to the standard expected of a G'day park and it has no dump point. Also, the public dump point is closed at the Tourist Info, so be prepared! We stayed over a weekend and the amenities were not cleaned. Location is good.
This is a lovely park in a great location, the sites are roomy and most have slabs, everything is clean and tidy and all the staff are very friendly and helpful.

For a g-day park,and you pay $40 a night and there is no dump point and you have to carry your sullage to a central point around the near the toilet block. The toilets and showers are clean and neat the camp kitchen is large and comfortable. One of the receptionist is a bit grumpy  when needing change for the washing machines.

Good location BUT cabin was a massive disappointment, sadly in need of major maintenance and cleaning. Front door ..unbelievable!! Front steps and porch littered and full of cobwebs. Hot water constantly dribbling from the shower. Couldn't even turn the cold tap on. Toaster and microwave dirty. Hotplate very temperamental Fridge freezer unusable. Ants in the kitchen area as well. A real disappointment as the park has such great potential
Had the unfortunate pleasure of staying in one of the cabins. The standard of cleanliness was appalling.  I would not recommend staying here.
Older, clean facilities. Somewhat disappointed we were placed next to the road in a virtually empty park when plenty of off road sites were available. Recommend. No.
Mainly cabins and apartments so it’s like a delux caravan park. G day discount. Straight out to small beach cove coffee shop at end wharf and great history plaques on walkways
Small. Lack of wifi connection.  Not fancy.

very disappointed as we have stayed at g’day parks during our 3wks long travel.this is by far the only dirty one,we didn’t mind that it is old.sheets seem to have been reused-if so, please charge more more washing instead of having people use sheets others have already slept on.unfolded blankets on the bunk beds at first entry.cabins don’t seem to be maintained

We stayed in a cabin (C20) with a "sea view", a bit of a stretch but yes you could see the ocean. The cabin is a bit tired, internal security door latch not working, (couldn't lock screen at all) with leaking kitchen tap spilling over the bench top dirty curtains. The bath room is very small and probably needs a little more attention in the cleaning department. Bed was comfortable but made the room very cramped and had to shuffle sideways to get out of bedroom. Ceiling fans very dirty. Some utensils were missing. Only minor issues in the scheme of things, but paid a premium fee. On arrival no " welcome to our park etc..." and upon leaving no question until I prompted about the quality of our stay. It was basic accommodation, pleasant enough, would I stay there again, probably not, would I recommend the cabin accommodation, probably not. We stayed 5 nights

Nice quiet little park, staff were helpfull, amenities clean.|One review was critical of the dump point BUT the dump point at the local tourist information centre has been out of action for some time, the other park charges $10 to use that. So the staff here a doing their best to help guests out.|No complaints here.

Lady in reception was rude - barely acknowledged me before I was handed me a highlighted sheet for the park with nothing further... no welcome or enjoy your stay. Went to the site highlighted on the map only to find it was occupied- went back to reception and had her condescendingly say to her offsider to and check "she" has the correct site, then if they are on the wrong site they probably won't want to move... Turned out I can read a map and numbers on the site... Ended up on a site too small for our caravan as our hitch was in the roadway and our rear nearly touching the back fence... Poor customer service- if you hate your job that much that you can't be pleasant you should probably find a new job
Also no proper dump point...  just empty it down and open drain was what reception said... pretty gross

These cabins are well-equipped and spotless.  They are positioned at the edge of town but it's a small town so it's not inconvenient.  It is a very quiet are with no passing traffic so it's nice.
The cabins are well equipped and comfortable.

If you are driving through or want to stay for a few days at this nice town, these cabins are good value and worth booking.

Very friendly staff do the cleaning.

Cabin had no bathroom which was not clearly stated on G'day parks, it smells like mold, has no lock on the screen door to even let in a breeze to get rid of the stink, the old laminate cabinets are broken and disgusting, the oven is so old I'm afraid to use it, and there's cobwebs everywhere in the room.
Stayed one night clean well maintained park

Used to stay here as a youngster over 40 years ago. It's changed a little bit, but was still recognisable. We had an ensuite site, which was great! Good facilities, though some are showing their age. Safe swimming beach in close proximity. Impressive coastline nearby. My wife, who is a Tasmanian native, and seeing the Eyre Peninsula for the first time, was impressed. Sorry, I forgot to take a picture of the park.
Had a good stay, close to town

What a disappointment.  Ok the price and the concrete slab where ok. BUT .. . . The public dump point is next to the dishwasher sink. AND our site was opposite and the smell and mess was a disgrace. We have been before but the new people have no idea 😕

The receptionist was not the friendliest I've ever met and then when we found our site it was right outside the entrance to the dunnies and the aroma that wafted in when the wind changed was bloody awful. Apart from that the camp kitchen was well kept and easy walking to the beach and shop.

We had the 2 bedroom cabin at this CP
Clean and cosy and local Campers were friendly
Close by to beach which is couple mins walking distance
Overflow unpowered sites are the most scenic, secluded and have grass
Nice caravan park short walk to bakery and supermarket also by the beach

Second year visiting and staying in Waterloo park. Parks facilities are a little bit older but it’s kept clean, park is in immaculate condition and the service is fantastic.
Highly recommend 👍

Great location. We were put in cabin 3 which must be one of the oldest cabins compared to the photos I've seen in other reviews. The old decor was not an issue, and the bed was very comfy and clean, but  it was very dirty in the bathroom corners around the shower and around window sills - looks like a built up over months which could have been sorted with a quick vacuum.

Toilet and shower facilities are very unclean and appear not to have been cleaned for two days. We have actually mopped the facility floors now several times given the unclean state , including toilet paper strewn across the floors. Women’s toilets actually ran out of toilet paper today. Office lights stated reception open but had to use intercom button outside of reception to get paper sorted. Rubbish bins are full and not been emptied today. Laundry has one machine not working and a 2nd that seems to just take your coin and also not working.
We stayed at an unpowered site. Water is available at each site. Good WIFI connection all over the site. Amenities are clean and totally okay. Just a small way to the beach.

Neat, tidy and quiet spot. Close to everything in town.
Dog friendly. Nice spot.

Excellent location , staff are amazing , showers are great lovely and hot and great water pressure   Camp kitchen is fantastic
Best sour dough every Saturday just a stroll down the road and pub is an easy walk

Really disappointing we called into the cabin park and needed 3 rooms as we were rained out of our camp site only to be turned away because there was a 2 night minimum only pouring rain windy and needed a place to stay there was vacancy and 3 cabins would have been booked.

Location, amenities, staff and atmosphere all excellent. What a gem! I was especially impressed by the camp kitchens and the shower facilities. Each shower had sufficient hooks to hang everything up on, the water pressure great and the cubicles sufficient for the traditionally built! The laundry was well appointed. Everything was clean and well maintained. The dog friendly beach just a short walk…we loved it!
Good caravan park
An awesome park!
Great Cabin with a million dollar view of the Bay, everything you need was there and ready to go. Great place to stay :)
Lovely park, friendly owners, beautiful scenery would definitely come back
Enjoyed our time here.  The park is very clean, neat and tidy.  Location is perfect.
Parked a caravan for 3 days.
Very welcoming, close to shops and the beach.

First grass to camp on after crossing the Nullarbor, so nice. Best location on beach and in town. Great staff. Clean amenities. And you can charge your EV on the 15amp.
Perfect!
Only downfall is no dump point and the one in town is closed down so only other option is the other caravan park at $10 a dump which would have been great to know prior
The ladies and the gardener were extremely friendly and helpful always a great location the park is always well manicured. A great place to stay but book early as it is always booked out.
Friendly staff, beautiful surroundings, great location. A very peaceful atmosphere and relaxing stay.
Staff were rude and unhelpful.
Fantastic friendly service and great location near the beach and shops.

The location is perfect however,  I was disappointed to see the park encroach and undermine the adjoining sandhill I loved to camp alongside when staying here 30 years ago. Now it's been cut back and retained to accommodate more vans at it's expense. How is that consistent with the today's ' eco tourism' philosophy? They've jammed cabins, gas tanks, water tanks, and cut a road deep into the fragile sandhill. The park has lost its unique charm and peaceful ambience and has become an over- commercialised and overcrowded tourist park
Great spot, clean amenities and lovely people...we'll be back!

Well run park  has some drive-through sites modern cabins clean amenities close to town.
Friendly staff

What a fabulous park with lots of green grass and great size sites. We had ensuite site that was huge! Ensuite u could have a party in!! Staff friendly and very helpful pointing out best places to visit and to fish. Good facilities including stove in camp kitchen.

Fantastic caravan park, great staff, however  amenities could do with a "proper" daily clean. Location is spot on for basing yourself when touring Elliston and all it has to offer.

We stayed an extra 2 nights, 5 total as it was amazing weather for exploring the area.

They offer cabins, powered and unpowered sites, fish cleaning station and well set up camp kitchen with a jumping pillow for the kid's also.
Highly recommend if in the area.

The Team who looks after this site are engaging and are able to create connection with their guests. Service, is without a doubt, one of the best. As a regular visitor, we're remembered and addressed by name, and are anticipated of arrival times as to minimise any wait time to settle in. This really creates a strong feeling of being welcoming, thus a sense of loyal need to return to stay, which is exactly the case at hand. Of course, the location also plays a big part being in Elliston which is great little town on the foreshore receiving the freshest of southern air. A beautiful place to be in, reflected by the residents.
The reception lady was very kind and funny. Very peaceful caravan park, I had the shower for myself
Good location to beach front. Loads of cabins.  Nice grassed site with generous concrete slab and shady trees around.   Ablutions old but clean.
$150 for a cabin with no hot water. No refund given for that night. I asked for my money back for the second night. Cabin was worth about $100. I moved up the road got a brand new cabin for $155.

I have traveled from Adelaide to my nursing placement in Elliston and I highly recommend this place for the future nursing student. This caravan park has provided us a very comfortable and affordable cabin with friendly customer service! Thank you Michael and Maryann for looking after us while we were so far away from home!
Great park, near beach

Such a beautiful, quiet, relaxing park. There is a walkway to the beach from the park, and of a night time, you can hear the ocean.  I had an ensuite site, which was very large, and the ensuite is huge and very clean. The lady at reception was very friendly, welcoming, and helpful. I certainly stumbled onto a gem with this park and would definitely visit again.
Clean and well maintained cvan park close to the beach ,staff friendly and helpful, amenities well looked after, sites plenty big enough easy to get into
A good stopover town to slow down your pace during touring Eyre Peninsula. Good family run business. All the amenities are there and a fish clearing station.
Lovely pitch, great facilities, friendly staff
Great place right near the jetty and pub
It is a fabulous place. Loved the sculptures.

Just a short walk to the beach. This is a pet-friendly caravan park with powered and unpowered sites, as well as drive-through sites. We had an ensuite site and got reasonable speeds on using the free wifi. Staff were friendly and helpful. Elliston is a small country town with a great pub for evening meals.

Didnt look at rooms as had our caravan.
Sites are a good size, plenty of shady trees, shower toilets and laundry are in good condition.
Would stay again if going to Elliston.
Annette was a helpful and friendly Receptionist and advised me about local sightseeing highlights.

The staff were lovely and very helpful, a good quiet cheap caravan park. The amenities are a little old but spotlessly clean. Walking distance to shop, bakery, beach and a pub with great meals. Would definitely stay again.
Lovely place for a 1 night stop. Friendly owners. Clean amenities close to town.
Many a visit over the years and we pray for more
Old park but well kept. Ablutions very good. Plenty of hot water. Owners very nice. Would stay again.
Absolutely loved staying here. What a shame it wasn't longer as locals so nice.
Everything was wonderful experience scenery park was great amenities clean thank you for hospitality

We stayed in this caravan park in July 2023. Very spacious sites with plenty of green grass, walking distance to the beautiful bay and the walks with amazing views! Clean amenities. We will stay there again.
Great place Great people greate prices
